channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Over 1,000 high school students across San Diego County walk out for environment
item.description: More than 1,000 students across 19 high schools in San Diego County participated in a state-wide walkout on Friday, urging lawmakers to pass climate related legislation.
item.dc:creator: Chris Ponce, Fox 5

item.title: Trump ends trade talks with Canada over tariffs ad that Ontario premier now says he'll phase out
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced he’s ending “all trade negotiations” with Canada because of a television ad sponsored by one of its provinces that used the words of former President Ronald Reagan to criticize U.S. tariffs — prompting the province's leader to later pull the ad.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, SEUNG MIN KIM and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Newsom bails out Planned Parenthood with $140M to keep 100 clinics open after Trump cuts
item.description: California governor Gavin Newsom announces $140 million in state funding to keep 109 Planned Parenthood clinics open amid federal cuts and defunding efforts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congresswoman scammed in her home by fake cleaning crew, police say
item.description: Del. Eleanor Holmes Norton (D-D.C.) was scammed on Thursday by a group claiming to be a cleaning crew, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eleanor Holmes Norton, an 88-year-old Dem congressional delegate, scammed by phony 'cleaning crew' at DC home
item.description: A Washington, D.C., congressional delegate was targeted in a brazen credit card fraud scheme after suspects posed as maintenance workers to access her home and finances.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reagan Foundation becomes the latest US institution drawn into Donald Trump's controversies
item.description: The Ronald Reagan Presidential Foundation and Institute is drawing a volatile mix of blowback and praise for arguing that a Canadian government advertisement playing in U.S. markets misrepresented the 40th president’s words to blast President Donald Trump’s tariff policies.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and THALIA BEATY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US imposes sanctions on Colombia’s president and family members over drug trade allegations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration imposed sanctions Friday on Colombian President Gustavo Petro, his family and a member of his government over accusations of involvement in the global drug trade, sharply escalating tensions with the leftist leader of one of the closest U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to meet Chinese president next week for key trade deal: White House
item.description: President Trump is preparing for a high-stakes trip to Asia aimed at testing his reputation as a dealmaker, with a potential trade agreement with China on the line.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG and PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pentagon sends largest aircraft carrier to Caribbean amid boat strikes
item.description: Hegseth’s order comes as the Trump administration has been striking alleged drug-smuggling boats in the Caribbean Sea.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Inflation data unlikely to be released next month, White House says
item.description: The White House says there probably won't be an October inflation report due to the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Trump parachutes into key 2025 race as GOP aims to flip governor office in blue-leaning state
item.description: Trump headlines tele-rally for Jack Ciattarelli as New Jersey governor race tightens before early voting begins. Latest polls show narrowing gap with Mikie Sherrill.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As strikes on alleged drug boats grow, so do questions about their legality and goal
item.description: President Donald Trump says he has authority to carry out the strikes, but international experts are asking if the attacks are truly about countering narcotics or instead toppling Venezuela's Nicolás Maduro.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez and Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Mamdani laughs off criticism that his tax hike proposals will compel New Yorkers to flee
item.description: NYC mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani laughed about concerns over his proposed tax hikes during a podcast discussion about whether they would cause residents to flee.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New York AG Letitia James pleads not guilty to federal fraud charges
item.description: James told a crowd outside the courtroom their support has "strengthened my spirit, and it has anchored my soul.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: NY AG Letitia James to make first court appearance in mortgage fraud case
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — New York Attorney General Letitia James is set to make her first court appearance in a mortgage fraud case on Friday, the third adversary of President Donald Trump to face a judge on federal charges in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: White House East Wing demolished as Trump moves forward with ballroom construction, AP photos show
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The entire White House East Wing has been demolished as President Donald Trump moves forward with construction of a ballroom, according to photos taken Thursday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and JACQUELYN MARTIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP senator blocks Democratic bill to pay all federal employees during shutdown
item.description: Sen. Ron Johnson (R-Wis.) objected to a request by Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D-Md.) on Thursday for the Senate to immediately consider and pass a bill to pay all federal workers, members of the military and federal contractors during the 2025 government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Trump calls off plans to deploy federal troops to San Francisco
item.description: The president said Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang and Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff were among "great people" who urged him not to deploy the National Guard to the city.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate rejects bills to pay federal workers during government shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate on Thursday rejected dueling partisan bills to pay federal workers during the government shutdown, with both Republicans and Democrats deflecting blame as many employees are set to miss their first full paycheck at the end of this week.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zohran Mamdani responds to ‘racist attacks’ from opponent
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Democratic nominee for mayor Zohran Mamdani responded on Thursday to his opponent’s controversial remarks about 9/11, calling them “racist attacks.” In an interview with PIX on Politics host Dan Mannarino, Mamdani took an emotional tone, addressing former Gov.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Illinois politician Darren Bailey's son, family killed in crash
item.description: Republican candidate for Illinois governor Darren Bailey lost his son, daughter-in-law and two grandchildren in the crash.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams endorses Andrew Cuomo
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams endorsed independent candidate former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo as his successor on Thursday. During Wednesday night’s final mayoral debate, Cuomo was the only candidate who said he’d accept an endorsement from Adams when asked by moderators.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Henry Rosoff, NewsNation

item.title: Stephen A. Smith apologizes to Crockett over 'street verbiage' remark
item.description: Pundit Stephen A. Smith apologized this week for comments he made criticizing Rep. Jasmine Crockett (D-Texas) and her approach to politics.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: California leaders write letter to Hegseth, demand answers for artillery fire over I-5
item.description: CAMP PENDLETON, Calif. (FOX 5/KUSI) -- California Democrats sent a letter to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth on Wednesday regarding shrapnel that struck California Highway Patrol (CHP) vehicles during Vice President JD Vance’s visit to Camp Pendleton.
item.dc:creator: Chris Ponce, Fox 5

item.title: Democrats block government funding bill for 12th time after marathon floor protest
item.description: Senate Democrats voted Wednesday for a 12th time to block a House-passed bill to reopen the government and keep it funded through Nov. 21. Democrats blocked the funding measure — a clean continuing resolution that would prolong Biden-era funding levels — after liberal Sen. Jeff Merkley (D-Ore.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Behind closed doors, Illinois lawmakers discuss potential redistricting effort
item.description: CHICAGO — As the redistricting battle plays out across the county, political leaders in Illinois are staying quiet about their plans to redraw congressional maps here. But under intense pressure from democrats in Washington D.C., the debate is starting to spill out into public view.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Trump announces sanctions on Russian oil in push for ceasefire
item.description: Trump announced sanctions on Russian oil companies Wednesday, demanding that Russia agree to a ceasefire with Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Marty Hobe, NewsNation

item.title: DeSantis: Progressive to issue nearly $1 billion in credits to Florida policyholders
item.description: “Thanks to Commissioner (Michael) Yaworsky, he has secured almost a billion dollars in credits for Progressive auto insurance policyholders,” DeSantis said.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Anti-science bills hit statehouses, stripping away public health protections built over a century
item.description: More than 420 anti-science bills attacking longstanding public health protections – vaccines, milk safety and fluoride – have been introduced in statehouses across the U.S. this year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump pick to lead watchdog agency withdraws after offensive text messages revealed
item.description: President Trump’s pick to lead a federal watchdog agency withdrew from consideration Tuesday evening after his offensive text messages were made public and GOP senators revolted.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Senate Republicans go to White House in show of unity
item.description: As the government shutdown enters its fourth week, Senate Republicans are headed to the White House — not for urgent talks on how to end it, but for a display of unity with President Donald Trump as they refuse to negotiate on any Democratic demands.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: San Diego City Council ordinance limits participation in unauthorized federal operations
item.description: The San Diego City Council unanimously passed an ordinance requiring judicial warrants for federal enforcement to enter City-controlled non-public properties on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Chris Ponce, Fox 5

item.title: As deadline for Trump’s colleges compact looms, schools signal dissent
item.description: Of the original nine schools that received the Trump administration's Compact for Academic Excellence in Higher Education, the majority have indicated they are not planning on signing.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Nadworny, MPR News

item.title: As they go unpaid, Capitol Police are a daily reminder for lawmakers of shutdown pain
item.description: The more than 2,000 officers on the force missed their first full paycheck Oct. 10, leaving them to go without pay at a moment when the officials they protect face growing threats and violence.
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas, MPR News
